title: Medication adherence mental health

medication.text: Supports documenting the person’s understanding, perceived need, concerns, adverse-effect concerns, daily-routine fit, and questions during medicine review.
medication.rationale: Supports review of knowledge, beliefs and concerns for mental-health medication adherence; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting the person’s understanding, perceived need, concerns, adverse-effect concerns, daily-routine fit, and questions during medicine review.

source.title: Medicines adherence: involving patients in decisions about prescribed medicines and supporting adherence

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.1.19–1.1.23 — knowledge, beliefs, and concerns about medicines

medication.text: Supports documenting missed doses, stopping or restarting, intentional or practical barriers, and support preferences without generating an intervention.
medication.rationale: Supports non-judgemental adherence assessment and tailored support for mental-health medication adherence; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting missed doses, stopping or restarting, intentional or practical barriers, and support preferences without generating an intervention.

source.title: Medicines adherence: involving patients in decisions about prescribed medicines and supporting adherence

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.8 — non-judgemental adherence assessment and tailored support

medication.text: Supports documenting periodic review of knowledge, concerns, need, and clinician prescribing decisions.
medication.rationale: Supports periodic medicine review for mental-health medication adherence; no decision is generated.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports documenting periodic review of knowledge, concerns, need, and clinician prescribing decisions.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Medicine, indication, dose, schedule, missed doses, reasons, benefits, harms, capacity, risk, clinician decision and follow-up remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for mental-health medication adherence.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
